page: Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-1600s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz, utilizing a shared CPU allocation model. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 1600 GB NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory performance, with uncached large-block read and write memory bandwidth scoring in the top 25% tier at 258.36 GB/sec and 152.63 GB/sec, respectively. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is highly competitive, placing in the top 10% tier. However, multi-core CPU performance and database operations fall into the average tier. Large-model LLM prompt processing represents a bottleneck, registering in the bottom 25% tier for a 7B model, though text generation for the same model remains average.

From a resource density perspective, this configuration offers high memory and storage capacity relative to its 8 vCPUs, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation, which may introduce performance variability under sustained multi-core loads. This server is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, medium-sized databases, caching layers like Redis, and web serving environments that benefit from a large memory footprint and fast local NVMe storage.
description: The Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-1600s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 8 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC Milan architecture with a shared CPU allocation. It is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, and includes a bundled 1600 GB NVMe SSD. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU capabilities and strong uncached memory bandwidth, though multi-core performance and database operations align with mid-tier averages. Due to its shared CPU model, workloads requiring sustained, predictable multi-core processing may experience variability. However, the generous memory allocation and fast local storage make this server highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, caching systems, and database hosting where high memory-to-vCPU density is required.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-1600s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 8 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and a 1600 GB NVMe SSD, designed for memory-intensive and database workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-1600s-amd is an AMD EPYC Milan server with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1600 GB NVMe storage, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring AMD EPYC processors and high-capacity NVMe storage for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC Milan architecture with 8 vCPUs and shared CPU allocation
High memory density with 64.0 GB RAM providing 8.0 GB per core
Bundled 1600 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory bandwidth
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and web serving
